module1.clean_text:
HDJ RE-hospitalization report
The child [PATIENT] aged [AGE] from Chlef and residing in Oran, followed in our department since [DATE] for a primary immunodeficiency type "Wiskott Aldrich syndrome", complicated by dilation of the cylindrical DDB bronchi and chronic respiratory failure stage 1. Admitted on [DATE] for his treatment 53 immunoglobulins
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Wiskott Aldrich type DIP
Date of diagnosis: [DATE] of diagnosis: 04 years and 9 [AGE] diagnoses: Dilation of the cylindrical bronchi
Chronic respiratory failure stage 1
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics
-Male gender
-Repeated respiratory infections
-Several outbreaks of eczema. Clinic
-Anemic syndrome consisting of: mucocutaneous pallor. -Skin hemorrhagic syndrome such as petechiae spread to the face and trunk. -Eczema lesions on the face and lower limbs. Para-clinical:
-Bi cytopenia; anemia, thrombocytopenia
- Assessment of primary immunodeficiency: [DATE] Profound reduction in the expression of the WASp protein results in favor of DIP Wiskott Aldrich syndrome
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: [DATE] (0.8g/kg)
Last update made in [DATE] OF HOSPITALIZATIONS
ANAMNESIS [DATE] since the last visit: [DATE] no
On the respiratory level: wet cough, clear sputum,
Ocularly: Conjunctival hyperemia, following ocular trauma on [DATE] No hemorrhage
Preserved appetite
Under ambulatory oxygen therapy
Schooled
Processing in progress:
Azithromycin 1 day out of [DATE] 25mg/kg/d
Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day: 02 times/day
Pulmicort 1mg: 02 times/day
Nebulization with hypertonic salt serum
Respiratory physiotherapy done by the mother
Well-kept notebooks
Assessments made:
ECBC: Candida Albicans released on 02/15/2026
IgG rate: 02/08/2026: 8.24g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 03/12/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 27.1 kg [-1.1 DS] Height: 132 cm [-1.2 DS] BMI: 15.6 kg/m2 (-0.9) Normal
General condition preserved, conscious, T: 37.2
Skin examination: no cutaneous and mucosal hemorrhagic syndrome, dry skin, multiple scars on the lower limbs
Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 38c/min SPo2: 96% Under 1 liter of O2, slight signs of struggle. Thoracic keel deformity, clubbing
. Auscultation of polymorphic rales
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 98 bpm, Auscultation: no murmur/no added noises
Ophthalmic examination: Conjunctival hyperemia of the left eye, normal right eye
Abdominal examination: flexible transit preserved, no organomegaly
ENT examination: Presence of dental caries
Examination of other devices without anomalies
Paraclinical assessment upon admission: 03/12/2026
FNS: WBC: 7400 elements/ul (PNN: 5700 Lymph: 1200) Hb: 10.6 g/dl Platelet: 90,000 elements/ul
CRP: negative TQ: 15.6 Sec TP: 69.6% TCK: 30 Sec
Support:
-Hospitalization
- Conditioning, peripheral venous route
-Replacement immunotherapy: IV infusion at 0.8g/kg or 20g, to be administered over 4 hours (Intratect)
-Monitoring
- Ophthalmic opinion made: AV: Right and left eye: 10/10 Conjunctival hyperemia of the left eye with transparent cornea, tearing suggesting viral conjunctivitis, child placed on Tobrex eye drops and eye wash by SSI. The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident
Same day release
Continue the same treatment: bactrim antibiotic prophylaxis and itraconazole antifungal
Zithromax, Pulmicort
Physiotherapy and saline nebulizations
Hygiene-dietary advice and notebook keeping
Control appointment: 04/12/2026 for immunoglobulin treatment
To collect / To do:
Next immunoglobulin treatment appointment is scheduled for 04/14/2026
Plan an ECBC to recover before the next treatment
Plan a chest CT,
Schedule an abdominopelvic ultrasound
Schedule an echocardiogram
Stomato advice (dental caries)
Retrieve the health record
Sibling screening
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil

phenopacket.input:
HDJ RE-hospitalization report
The child Amroun Abdel Aziz, aged 09 from Chlef and residing in Oran, followed in our department since 01/11/2021 for a primary immunodeficiency type "Wiskott Aldrich syndrome", complicated by dilation of the cylindrical DDB bronchi and chronic respiratory failure stage 1. Admitted on 03/12/2026 for his treatment 53 immunoglobulins
History of illness:
Main diagnosis: Wiskott Aldrich type DIP
Date of diagnosis: 12/11/2020
Age of diagnosis: 04 years and 9 months
Associated diagnoses: Dilation of the cylindrical bronchi
Chronic respiratory failure stage 1
Diagnostic elements:
Anamnestics
-Male gender
-Repeated respiratory infections
-Several outbreaks of eczema. Clinic
-Anemic syndrome consisting of: mucocutaneous pallor. -Skin hemorrhagic syndrome such as petechiae spread to the face and trunk. -Eczema lesions on the face and lower limbs. Para-clinical:
-Bi cytopenia; anemia, thrombocytopenia
- Assessment of primary immunodeficiency: 12/29/2020, Profound reduction in the expression of the WASp protein results in favor of DIP Wiskott Aldrich syndrome
Immunoglobulin treatment at a rate of 0.8mg1/kg/4h at the SAP each month. Last dose received: 02/09/2026 (0.8g/kg)
Last update made in May 2025
HISTORY OF HOSPITALIZATIONS
ANAMNESIS 03/12/2026
Events since the last visit: 02/09/2026
Consultation: no
On the respiratory level: wet cough, clear sputum,
Ocularly: Conjunctival hyperemia, following ocular trauma on 03/10/2026
Hematologically: No hemorrhage
Preserved appetite
Under ambulatory oxygen therapy
Schooled
Processing in progress:
Azithromycin 1 day out of 02
Bactrim 25mg/kg/d
Itraconazole 05mg/kg/day: 02 times/day
Pulmicort 1mg: 02 times/day
Nebulization with hypertonic salt serum
Respiratory physiotherapy done by the mother
Well-kept notebooks
Assessments made:
ECBC: Candida Albicans released on 02/15/2026
IgG rate: 02/08/2026: 8.24g/l
CLINICAL EXAMINATION UPON ADMISSION: Date: 03/12/2026
Anthropometric measurements:
Weight: 27.1 kg [-1.1 DS] Height: 132 cm [-1.2 DS] BMI: 15.6 kg/m2 (-0.9) Normal
General condition preserved, conscious, T: 37.2
Skin examination: no cutaneous and mucosal hemorrhagic syndrome, dry skin, multiple scars on the lower limbs
Pleuropulmonary examination: FR: 38c/min SPo2: 96% Under 1 liter of O2, slight signs of struggle. Thoracic keel deformity, clubbing
. Auscultation of polymorphic rales
Cardiovascular examination: HR: 98 bpm, Auscultation: no murmur/no added noises
Ophthalmic examination: Conjunctival hyperemia of the left eye, normal right eye
Abdominal examination: flexible transit preserved, no organomegaly
ENT examination: Presence of dental caries
Examination of other devices without anomalies
Paraclinical assessment upon admission: 03/12/2026
FNS: WBC: 7400 elements/ul (PNN: 5700 Lymph: 1200) Hb: 10.6 g/dl Platelet: 90,000 elements/ul
CRP: negative TQ: 15.6 Sec TP: 69.6% TCK: 30 Sec
Support:
-Hospitalization
- Conditioning, peripheral venous route
-Replacement immunotherapy: IV infusion at 0.8g/kg or 20g, to be administered over 4 hours (Intratect)
-Monitoring
- Ophthalmic opinion made: AV: Right and left eye: 10/10 Conjunctival hyperemia of the left eye with transparent cornea, tearing suggesting viral conjunctivitis, child placed on Tobrex eye drops and eye wash by SSI. The immunoglobulin treatment passed without incident
Same day release
Continue the same treatment: bactrim antibiotic prophylaxis and itraconazole antifungal
Zithromax, Pulmicort
Physiotherapy and saline nebulizations
Hygiene-dietary advice and notebook keeping
Control appointment: 04/12/2026 for immunoglobulin treatment
To collect / To do:
Next immunoglobulin treatment appointment is scheduled for 04/14/2026
Plan an ECBC to recover before the next treatment
Plan a chest CT,
Schedule an abdominopelvic ultrasound
Schedule an echocardiogram
Stomato advice (dental caries)
Retrieve the health record
Sibling screening
Department resident: Dr Djoudi Department assistant: Dr Khelil
